ogTitle: Towan vs Towaninny South: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Towan and Towaninny South.
heading: Towan vs Towaninny South — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Towan, Victoria and Towaninny South, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Towan has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Towaninny South (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Towan or Towaninny South safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Towan has a flood risk score of 23/100, while Towaninny South scores 52/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Towan scores 61/100 for bushfire risk. Towaninny South scores 30/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Towan or Towaninny South?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Towan has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.25% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
